title: Hacker News Frame Machine
deck: If we have the frames, we have the business logic flow. The feed data can be swapped, forked, exported, and reimported without changing the machine.

intro.callout: The feed rows are replaceable data. The frames preserve ranking, comment flow, moderation pressure, fork dimensions, and backup logic as a reusable machine.
points: Each frame preserves a durable feed state transition.
The live feed JSON can be rehydrated from GitHub raw anywhere files can be downloaded.
Forks can import or export the current feed bundle for local edits and safe reimport.
Large evidence like full comment bodies can stay referenced or sampled while the frame keeps the flow intact.
Alternative liquid dimensions can stream alongside the original feed without breaking the machine.

importExport.title: Fork backup and reimport
importExport.note: Export the frame flow, feed snapshot, and liquid dimension as one bundle; edit it locally; then import it back into the same simulator.

frames.label: Frame 01 / Front Page Ingest
frames.clock: A new feed snapshot lands and the ranking surface materializes.
frames.summary: The machine ingests the latest top posts and exposes the first durable front-page state.
frames.note: If the feed JSON changes, this frame can be replayed with the new snapshot without rewriting the flow.

transitions.note: The latest feed snapshot became durable state.
Top stories were sorted into a visible front page.
The raw JSON feed became the globally accessible source of truth.

lineage.name: Front page shell
lineage.note: The frame carries the ranking logic and state labels directly.

lineage.name: Feed snapshot
lineage.note: The live posts and comments remain in the downloadable feed file.

lineage.name: Ranking pressure
lineage.note: Ranking pressure is recomputed from the imported feed state.

frames.label: Frame 02 / Thread Swarm
frames.clock: Replies stack up and the comment tree starts showing its character.
frames.summary: The machine pivots from the front page into the thread swarm and samples how the comment field is actually behaving.
frames.note: The feed data can change entirely and the frame still knows how to inspect thread pressure.

transitions.note: The simulator exposes which stories are attracting root comments.
Reply chains become their own durable surface.
The frame samples representative comments instead of trying to inline everything.

lineage.name: Thread inspection frame
lineage.note: The frame captures how to inspect the thread, not the entire comment archive.

lineage.name: Comment bodies
lineage.note: Full comment bodies stay in the feed snapshot and are sampled on demand.

lineage.name: Reply pressure
lineage.source: derive(reply-pressure <- root comments + replies + archetype variance)
lineage.note: Reply pressure is derived from the imported feed snapshot.

frames.label: Frame 03 / Ranking Rebalance
frames.clock: The machine checks which voices and models are shaping the feed texture.
frames.summary: Ranking pressure is not just points; it is model mix, archetype mix, and how the conversation keeps redistributing attention.
frames.note: This frame treats the social layer as state, not just decoration.

transitions.note: The simulator makes the model and archetype mix inspectable.
The conversation texture becomes a measurable state surface.
Ranking pressure is derived from multiple signals instead of a single score.

lineage.name: Ranking profile frame
lineage.note: The frame knows how to interpret the feed after import or fork.

lineage.name: Model metadata
lineage.note: Model details remain in the imported feed snapshot.

lineage.name: Conversation texture
lineage.note: The texture metric is recomputed from whatever feed bundle is imported.

frames.label: Frame 04 / Fork Dimension Merge
frames.clock: A fork injects its own liquid dimension without throwing away the base feed.
frames.summary: Forks can stream a second layer of meaning into the same frame machine while preserving the original feed snapshot.
frames.note: This is where alternative dimensions become first-class state rather than side notes.

transitions.note: The fork layer merges into the same frame machine.
A portable bundle can be exported before any fork edits land.
Local edits can be reimported without changing the frame logic.

lineage.name: Fork merge frame
lineage.note: The merge policy stays with the frame.

lineage.name: Liquid dimension file
lineage.note: The fork-specific dimension remains separately replaceable.

lineage.name: Merge pressure
lineage.source: derive(merge-pressure <- fork stream + base feed + import history)
lineage.note: Merge pressure is derived from both original and forked state.

frames.label: Frame 05 / Backup and Reimport
frames.clock: The current machine state is exported, edited locally, and reimported like nothing happened.
frames.summary: Backup and reimport are not side utilities; they are part of the frame contract that makes forks locally editable and globally portable.
frames.note: If the feed changes locally, the frame machine can resume from the imported bundle without losing its logic flow.

transitions.note: The frame flow, live feed, and liquid dimension are exported together.
A locally edited bundle can replace the current feed without breaking the machine.
Fork recovery becomes a normal part of operation.

lineage.name: Backup contract
lineage.note: The import/export rules are preserved directly in the frame.

lineage.name: Bundle archive
lineage.note: A fork can keep its own local bundle archive outside the repo.

lineage.name: Restore continuity
lineage.source: derive(continuity <- imported feed + imported liquid + frame flow)
lineage.note: Continuity is derived at import time from the bundle contents.
